chart.title: Per capita electricity generation
chart.subtitle: Annual average electricity generation per person, measured in kilowatt-hours.
chart.citation: Ember (2026); Energy Institute - Statistical Review of World Energy (2025); Population based on various sources (2024)

descriptionKey: Per capita figures are calculated by dividing total values by the population of the country or region. Population data is constructed by Our World in Data, based on [various sources](https://ourworldindata.org/grapher/population).
Per capita electricity - kWh.descriptionProcessing: - Electricity data from 2000 onwards (and from 1990 onwards for European countries, including Turkey) comes from Ember. Earlier data comes from the Energy Institute.
Per capita electricity - kWh.citationShort: Ember (2026); Energy Institute - Statistical Review of World Energy (2025); Population based on various sources (2024) – with major processing by Our World in Data
Per capita electricity - kWh.citationLong: Ember (2026); Energy Institute - Statistical Review of World Energy (2025); Population based on various sources (2024) – with major processing by Our World in Data. “Total electricity generation per person” [dataset]. Ember, “Yearly Electricity Data Europe”; Ember, “Yearly Electricity Data”; Energy Institute, “Statistical Review of World Energy”; Various sources, “Population” [original data].
